summary: Large decomp/matching PR centered on almost completing src/melee/mn/mngallery.c, with smaller exact-match cleanups in gm_1601, grcorneria, and lbaudio_ax. The decomp.dev report credited 19 new matches, +6052 matched code bytes, and +24 matched data bytes; fn_802590C4 remained an unmatched-but-improved mngallery item at 99.19%. The PR also tightened headers by replacing several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with typed HSD_GObj, Vec3, u8 pointer, bool, and void signatures.

key_files.role: Primary implementation work: added gallery render/camera setup, MTHP playback startup/skip handling, menu input state machine, selection animation helpers, GObj construction, archive section loading, text and camera setup.
Replaced unknown gallery prototypes with typed signatures and added baselib forward declarations.
Implemented four game-mode helpers for time formatting and stage unlock checks.
Updated gm_1601 prototypes from int/void* placeholders to u8, u8*, and bool types.
Implemented grCorneria_801DEC94 as a Vec3 blast-zone proximity check.
Reshaped three audio functions to finish exact matches: GObj creation control flow, PAD_STACK/return inversion, and a void mask-marking helper.
Changed lbAudioAx_80026F2C prototype from u64 return to void.

what_changed: Implemented 9 fully matched mngallery functions listed by the author: mnGallery_8025896C, mnGallery_80258A08, mnGallery_80258BC4, mnGallery_80258DBC, fn_80258ED0, mnGallery_802591BC, mnGallery_80259604, mnGallery_8025963C, and mnGallery_80259868.
Added a near-match implementation of fn_802590C4 in mngallery.c; the bot report says it improved from 0.00% to 99.19%, not 100%.
mngallery.c gained many concrete baselib/menu/library includes plus a new static pointer mnGallery_804D6C88 alongside the existing mnGallery_804D6C8C movie buffer pointer.
mnGallery_8025896C became a render callback that only clears/draws the SObj view when the gallery state is 3.
mnGallery_80258A08 now allocates and configures an orthographic HSD_CObj camera with viewport, scissor, eye, interest, near/far, ortho bounds, object attachment, and GX link setup.
mnGallery_80258BC4 starts one of two MTHP movie modes, creates the movie GObj/SObj, configures dimensions/offsets, switches audio state around lbMthp_8001F410, and stores playback state in userdata.
mnGallery_80258DBC handles movie progress/skip logic using all four controller trigger fields, lbMthp status calls, gmMainLib_8046B0F0.xC, and cleanup of the movie GObj after skip/end.
fn_80258ED0 implements the gallery menu input state machine: cooldown handling, Back navigation cleanup, Up/Down selection, Start/A entering movie playback, and SIS text animation IDs 0xC7/0xC8.
mnGallery_802591BC syncs child selector GObj transforms from the parent joint, animates selected/unselected materials, and selects texture animation frames by index.

mnGallery_80259604 zeroes the observed gallery userdata fields; mnGallery_8025963C constructs the root gallery GObj, two child GObjs, user data, animation data, and procs; mnGallery_80259868 loads archive sections, enters menu 0x1A, creates input proc, text, and camera.
gm_801636D8 and gm_80163A3C now format stored frame counts into hour/minute/second/centisecond u8 outputs using 60 FPS conversion and the 99.0f * remainder / 59.0f formula.
gm_80163838 now totals frame counts across 0x19 entries, fails if any gmMainLib_8015D6BC check fails, and returns the aggregate time split through optional u8 pointers.
gm_80164430 now checks whether a stage is unlocked by converting the stage with Stage_8022519C, looking up unlock bits in lbl_803B790C, and testing the bitfield from gmMainLib_8015EDA4.
grCorneria_801DEC94 now checks a Vec3 against blast-zone offsets with a 30.0f margin when z > -30.0f and returns s32 1 or 0.

lbAudioAx_800263E8 was control-flow reshaped from goto labels into a nested arg3 < 0x83D60 path that creates an HSD_GObj, allocates userdata, initializes userdata/proc, calls fn_80025FAC, and otherwise returns NULL.
lbAudioAx_80026510 gained PAD_STACK(8) and an inverted final count test that returns false when count == 0 and true otherwise.
lbAudioAx_80026F2C changed from returning u64 to returning void, removed the pointer-walk variable, and writes -1 directly into lbl_804337C4[i] when mask bits are set.
Headers were updated to replace generic unknown prototypes with evidence-backed typed signatures in mngallery.h, gm_1601.h, grcorneria.h, and lbaudio_ax.h.
smart_moves: The PR used narrow anonymous local structs to model only the observed userdata/layout fields needed for matching mngallery code, avoiding premature full struct naming while still replacing UNK_PARAMS in public prototypes.
Header cleanup was paired with implementation work, so discovered signatures propagated immediately to callers and future decomp work.

Control-flow shaping was used deliberately: lbAudioAx_800263E8 was rewritten around a positive threshold check, lbAudioAx_80026510 inverted its final condition, gm_80164430 used a found label, and fn_802590C4 used pointer arithmetic/do-while cleanup to chase MWCC codegen.
The author documented that fn_802590C4 was still a hard near-match instead of presenting it as finished; the bot report confirmed the exact state at 99.19%.
Compiler and stack-layout nudges were localized with pragmas and PAD_STACK rather than spread through unrelated code.
Repeated frame-to-time formatting logic in gm_1601 was typed as u8 outputs and bool success/failure, which clarified semantics and improved matching.
lbaudio_ax cleanup recognized that lbAudioAx_80026F2C's computed mask is consumed for side effects only, making the void return type part of the match.
decomp_lessons: Do not leave prototypes as int/void*/UNK when the implementation reveals exact ABI-relevant types; bool vs int, void vs u64, Vec3* vs UNK_PARAMS, and u8* output parameters all affected these matches.

Near-matching a difficult MWCC function may require a combination of pragmas, stack padding, volatile/local variables, pointer walking, and exact cleanup ordering, but even extensive tactics may still leave a fuzzy item.
For menu code with HSD_GObj user data, local anonymous structs are a useful interim tactic when field offsets are known but durable semantic names are not.

MTHP menu playback setup in this gallery path uses a preallocated 0x271000 buffer, creates a GObj/SObj, toggles lbAudioAx_80024E50 around lbMthp_8001F410, and cleans up the movie GObj when playback is skipped or done.
The gallery menu state machine depends on both menu-level inputs from Menu_GetAllInputs and raw HSD_PadCopyStatus trigger aggregation for movie skipping.

Stage unlock helpers should convert stage IDs with Stage_8022519C before comparing against unlock tables such as lbl_803B790C; stages not found in the unlockable table are treated as available in gm_80164430.
Blast-zone proximity helpers can often be typed by comparing sibling functions: grCorneria_801DEC94 takes Vec3* and returns s32 just like the nearby grCorneria_801DEC08 style.
When matching side-effect-only mask functions, direct global array indexing can produce different codegen than pointer increment loops, and removing an unused return may be necessary.
naming_conventions: Preserved project address-based symbol names where semantics remain unknown: mnGallery_8025896C, fn_80258ED0, fn_802590C4, gm_801636D8, lbl_803B790C.
Kept subsystem prefixes and capitalization consistent: mnGallery_, gm_, grCorneria_, lbAudioAx_, Stage_, HSD_.
Functions with unclear semantics retained the fn_ prefix, while subsystem-known functions retained their module prefix.
Unknown struct fields stayed as unk offsets/pads rather than invented names, while public function parameter types were improved when the evidence was strong.
Header prototypes keep the repository's address-comment format such as /* 25896C */ and /* 026F2C */.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma dont_inline on around mnGallery_80258D50 and mnGallery_80259604.
Used #pragma optimization_level 2, #pragma opt_strength_reduction on, and #pragma opt_propagation off around fn_802590C4.

Used PAD_STACK in several sensitive functions, including PAD_STACK(0x10) in mnGallery render/input helpers, PAD_STACK(24) in mnGallery_802591BC, PAD_STACK(0x38) in mnGallery_80259868, and PAD_STACK(8) in lbAudioAx_80026510.
Matched codegen by changing lbAudioAx_800263E8 from goto-based early exits to a nested positive condition with a final return NULL.
Matched lbAudioAx_80026510 by spelling the final return as if count == 0 return false; return true.
Matched lbAudioAx_80026F2C by making it void and using lbl_804337C4[i] direct indexing instead of an incrementing int* plus returned mask.
Used a goto found label in gm_80164430 to preserve the table-search control-flow shape.
fn_802590C4 used explicit store/data variables, char* read_base, zero initialized from i, and a do-while loop over two child GObjs, but remained a 99.19% fuzzy match.
The author investigated MWCC/register-coloring behavior externally for fn_802590C4; a collaborator pointed to a fully matched MWCC tree as a possible reference.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code 56.15% (+0.16%, +6052 bytes) and matched data 37.51% (+24 bytes), with 19 new matches.
Confirmed full matches for the listed gm_1601, grcorneria, lbaudio_ax, and 9 mngallery functions, while fn_802590C4 remained unmatched but improved to 99.19%.
Reported one small unmatched-item regression in grcorneria .sdata2, from 20.48% to 20.00%; no human discussion of this regression appears in the provided slice.
Suggested a fully matched MWCC version at https://git.wuffs.org/MWCC/tree/?h=main as a possible reference for the author's fn_802590C4/MWCC coloring problem.
No formal review comments or requested changes are present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: fn_802590C4 MWCC coloring 99.19 opt_propagation opt_strength_reduction read_base gobjs

gm_801636D8 gm_80163838 frame count hours minutes seconds 99.0f 59.0f
